report_name: Operation Endgame: Global Law Enforcement Disrupts SocGholish, Cleans 15,000 Infected Websites

threat_actors.name: Evil Corp
threat_actors.description: A Russian cybercrime group identified as the operator of the SocGholish malware delivery network. The group functions as a major initial access broker, facilitating ransomware and espionage campaigns by selling access gained through SocGholish.

attack_patterns.name: Drive-by Compromise
attack_patterns.description: SocGholish operators compromised legitimate websites (WordPress, Joomla, Drupal) to inject malicious JavaScript, which then infected visitors via a drive-by compromise model.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution
attack_patterns.description: The attack relies on social engineering, tricking the user into downloading and executing a malicious file disguised as a browser update.

attack_patterns.name: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: Users are lured by a fake browser update prompt to download and execute a malicious ZIP archive containing a JScript loader.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript
attack_patterns.description: Malicious JavaScript was injected into legitimate websites to profile visitors and serve fake update lures. The initial payload was also a JScript loader.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The initial SocGholish loader, Gholoader, communicates with a C2 server to download and execute second-stage payloads like ransomware and RATs.

malware.name: SocGholish
malware.description: A JavaScript-based malware loader distributed via drive-by compromise on legitimate websites using fake browser update lures. It serves as an initial access broker, delivering various second-stage payloads.

malware.name: LockBit
malware.description: A ransomware family delivered as a second-stage payload by the SocGholish malware.

malware.name: RansomHub
malware.description: A ransomware family delivered as a second-stage payload by the SocGholish malware.

malware.name: Dridex
malware.description: A banking trojan delivered as a second-stage payload by the SocGholish malware.

malware.name: Raspberry Robin
malware.description: A malware loader delivered as a second-stage payload by the SocGholish malware.

malware.name: AsyncRAT
malware.description: A remote access trojan delivered as a second-stage payload by the SocGholish malware.

tools.name: NetSupport RAT
tools.description: A legitimate remote access tool delivered as a second-stage payload by SocGholish to enable remote control of infected systems.

campaigns.name: Operation Endgame
campaigns.description: An international law enforcement operation that successfully disrupted the infrastructure of the SocGholish malware network, involving the takedown of C2 servers and the cleaning of thousands of compromised websites.
campaigns.objective: Disrupt the SocGholish malware delivery network.

identities.name: WordPress Websites
identities.description: A class of approximately 14,971 compromised websites running the WordPress CMS, which were used as a distribution platform for the SocGholish malware.

identities.name: Joomla Websites
identities.description: A class of websites running the Joomla CMS that were compromised and used to distribute the SocGholish malware.

identities.name: Drupal Websites
identities.description: A class of websites running the Drupal CMS that were compromised and used to distribute the SocGholish malware.

infrastructure.name: SocGholish C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A network of 106 command-and-control servers that formed the backbone of the SocGholish botnet. This infrastructure was seized or sinkholed during Operation Endgame.

infrastructure.name: SocGholish Distribution Network
infrastructure.description: A network of approximately 15,000 compromised websites (including WordPress, Joomla, and Drupal sites) used to host malicious JavaScript and fake update lures to distribute the SocGholish malware.

indicators.name: SocGholish Fake Update ZIP Download
indicators.pattern:
title: SocGholish Fake Update ZIP Download
id: 2b9f3c1a-4d7e-4b8a-9c1d-8e7f6a5b4c3d
status: experimental
description: Detects the download of ZIP archives commonly named as fake browser updates, a TTP of the SocGholish malware.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/15000-wordpress-websites-cleaned-up-in-socgholish-botnet-takedown/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1189
level: high
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|endswith:
      - '/Chrome_update.zip'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the download of ZIP archives commonly named as fake browser updates, a known TTP of the SocGholish malware.

indicators.name: SocGholish JScript Loader Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: SocGholish JScript Loader Execution
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-4a3b-2c1d-0e9f8a7b6c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of a JScript file via wscript.exe, a common execution method for the SocGholish loader.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/15000-wordpress-websites-cleaned-up-in-socgholish-botnet-takedown/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1059.007
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wscript.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '.js'
      - '.jse'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of a JScript file via wscript.exe, a common execution method for the SocGholish loader after a user is tricked into opening the malicious ZIP file.
